title: Excessive side margins affect responsiveness
author: michellestrey
body:
Hi there! 👋

I noticed that on certain screen resolutions like 1366x768, the homepage displays very wide side margins, which limits the usable content area. This affects readability and the overall user experience, especially on smaller laptop screens.

It might be helpful to consider adjusting the layout or applying more responsive design principles for better adaptation across devices.

Here's a screenshot for reference:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2256d8a9-1bb6-49ca-a48a-1a282e2d8388)

Thanks for the amazing project! 🙌

title: RSS feed is broken
author: niLPotential
body: I was trying to subscribe via RSS but the xml was broken. It turns out that under `src/lib/feed.js`, `Feed.node()` does not close the `<item>` tag properly. The tag at the end is another opening one, instead of closing. Hopefully fixing that solves the issue.

title: Typo on the website
author: PesBandi
body:
There's a typo on [this page](https://www.30secondsofcode.org/js/s/hash-sha-256/#:~:text=As%20the%20promise-,reslves,-to%20an%20ArrayBuffer).

In the sentence "*As the promise reslves to an `ArrayBuffer`, you will need to read the data using `DataView.prototype.getUint32()`.*", "*reslvs*" was probably supposed to be "*resolves*".

title: Terminology issue in blog
author: kamtoeddy
body:
Hi maintainers,

Going through the interview prep. resource on [variable hoisting](https://github.com/30-seconds/30-seconds-of-code/blob/master/content/snippets/js/s/variable-hoisting.md), I noticed that the first sentence of the first paragraph had an issue.

It goes "Before your JavaScript code is executed, it is first parsed and `compiled`". My problem here is *`compiled`*. JavaScript is an interpreted language.

Thanks

title: fix: Unnecessary parameter in Queue class methods
author: kevinweejh
body:
Hello maintainers/contributors,

While going through the [JavaScript Data Structures - Queue](https://www.30secondsofcode.org/js/s/data-structures-queue/) page, I found a couple of oversights in the markdown content, specifically in the file located at content/snippets/js/s/data-structures-queue.md.

On lines 38 and 42, the implementation of the `dequeue()` and `peek()` methods do not use the `item` parameter. This is because unlike a priority queue, a typical FIFO-based queue will dequeue strictly from the start of the queue, same with the peeking method.

To fix this, the `item` parameter needs to be removed from both the `dequeue()` and `peek()` methods in lines 38 and 42 respectively.

I have searched through the open issues and found no duplicates of this issue. As such, may I work on this issue?

Once again, thank you for maintaining this resource! Let me know if I should go ahead and create a PR to fix this oversight.

title: Typo in the event-loop-explained.md snippet
author: kevinweejh
body:
Hello maintainers/contributors,

While going through the [Event Loop Explained](https://www.30secondsofcode.org/js/s/event-loop-explained/) page, I found a typo in the markdown content, specifically in the file located at content/snippets/js/s/event-loop-explained.md.

On line 96, the content reads:

`10. The Event Loop checks the Microtask Queue. As it’s not empty, it executes the first Microtask, which is the callback of Promise.prototype.then() that was queued in step 10.`

However, the callback for Promise.prototype.then() was queued in step 9, not step 10. For reference, step 9 states:

`9. Promise.prototype.then() is pushed to the Call Stack and executed. This creates a new entry for its callback function in the Microtask Queue.`

To fix this, the end of the sentence in step 10 should be corrected to "... that was queued in step 9."

I have searched through the open issues and found no duplicates of this issue. As such, may I work on this issue?

Thank you for maintaining this resource! Let me know if I should go ahead and create a PR to fix this typo.

title: passive-scroll-listener-performance has obsolete/incorrect advice
author: rebelmusic
body:
Hello there, this is the page in question: https://www.30secondsofcode.org/js/s/passive-scroll-listener-performance/

According to the specifications, adding passive: true to scroll is not necessary:

https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/EventTarget/addEventListener#improving_scrolling_performance_with_passive_listeners

title: New snippet ideas
author: caeltarifa
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/30-seconds/30-seconds-of-code/discussions/1925

Scatter plot on linear regression, less code and effort

Originally posted by **cael** May 30, 2023 This snippet would show how to plot a linear regression in few lines by Seaborn over Matplotlib. By the example, it illustrates the difference between both libraries doing the same, highlighting the simpleness of Seaborn for scatter plots. Seaborn's higher-level function and integration with underlying Matplotlib functionality provides a direct function of linear regression. Without using third-party libraries to perform the calculation, Seaborn makes it more convenient for creating advanced statistical plots with less code and effort. The dataset illustrated on the plot below shows the comparative that belongs to Seaborn and Matplotlib, respectively. ``` # Create a scatter plot with regression lines calculated using Seaborn sns.lmplot(x="sepal_length", y="sepal_width", hue="species", data=iris) plt.show() ``` ![image](https://github.com/30-seconds/30-seconds-of-code/assets/23003922/de084d45-2cb0-44fa-9422-ee520f9b6e42) ``` # Create a scatter plot using Matplotlib plt.scatter(iris["sepal_length"], iris["sepal_width"], c=iris["species"].cat.codes) plt.xlabel('sepal_length') plt.ylabel('sepal_width') # Calculate and plot regression lines for each category for species, group in iris.groupby("species"): x = group["sepal_length"] y = group["sepal_width"] m, b = np.polyfit(x, y, 1) plt.plot(x, m * x + b, label=species) ``` ![image](https://github.com/30-seconds/30-seconds-of-code/assets/23003922/487b7149-474c-4020-98bf-5f3090454f95)
